summary: Broken Link in Contributing.md: "Guidelines for Setting Up a Local Workspace" Leads to 404 - page not found

description.text:
### **Problem Description  **

- The link **"guidelines for setting up a local workspace."** in the `Contributing.md` file is broken.
- Clicking on it results in a **404 - Page Not Found** error.
- The issue occurs because the link is outdated or incorrect.

### **Steps to Reproduce**

1. Open the repository and navigate to the `Contributing.md` file.
2. Locate the **"guidelines for setting up a local workspace."** link.
3. Click on the link.
4. Observe that it leads to a **404 - Page Not Found** error.

### **A Fix Suggestion**

- Update the broken link with the correct URL: `[correct-link-here]`.
- If possible, verify that the correct link works properly before updating.
- A pull request (PR) can be submitted to update the `Contributing.md` file with the correct link.

summary: Error message when adding a new entry with an escaped opening curly bracket should be improved.

description.text:
**Is your suggestion for improvement related to a problem? Please describe.**
When creating a new BibTeX entry in JabRef where an author has an umlaut in their name, it might happen that accidentally, the opening curly bracket is escaped instead of the parenthesis. This results in an exception that the file could not be saved. Unfortunately, the stack trace is directly shown without highlighting the character that resulted in the exception (in this case, the closing curly bracket would be falsely required by the stack trace to be escaped too). 

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Highlighting the character in the stack trace helps quickly identify the issue.

User now can be able to automatically change their file name when they change their entry data, by just click the checkbox in the preferences ->linked file -> auto rename files if entry changes, and the auto rename functionality will be started, and also when the user change the entry data, the citation key. Also will automatically change, so they don't need to click the generate button manually,(This automatically change citation key functionality is initiated regardless of the checkbox of the auto rename files functionality is clicked) ### Mandatory checks - [x] I own the copyright of the code submitted and I licence it under the [MIT license](https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/blob/main/LICENSE) - [x] Change in `CHANGELOG.md` described in a way that is understandable for the average user (if change is visible to the user) - [x] Tests created for changes (if applicable) - [x] Manually tested changed features in running JabRef (always required) - [x] Screenshots added in PR description (for UI changes) - [x] [Checked developer's documentation](https://devdocs.jabref.org/): Is the information available and up to date? If not, I outlined it in this pull request. - [x] [Checked documentation](https://docs.jabref.org/): Is the information available and up to date? summary: Tag-Based Selection for Entry fields in Preferences

description.text:
This PR Fixes #12550

This PR replaces the existing text-based field selection with a tag-based selection format, similar to the implementation in the Autocompletion settings.

### Changes Made

- Converted text-based text input to TagsField components, allowing users to select fields dynamically instead of relying on static text labels.
- Improved layout alignment to ensure text and tags are displayed correctly.
- Adjusted spacing and padding in HBox to prevent label truncation or misalignment.
- Ensured that the new TagsField selection method works consistently with other settings sections.

### Screenshots after Fix Applied
![new](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d62ee062-013f-4247-8be6-a6c459c037c6)


### Mandatory checks

UI:Add option to disable automatic file renaming on entry changes This PR adds a new user preference option to control whether files should be automatically renamed when entry details change: (below the linked file name convention is the button I added, auto rename files if entry data changed) FilesOnEntryChange (default: false) •Added new boolean preference in FilePreferences: autoRename •Added checkbox in Preferences > Linked Files settings to toggle this option •Modified AutomaticFileRenamer to respect this preference setting •Updated all relevant tests to accommodate this new preference This change gives users more control over when files are renamed, addressing concerns about unexpected file system changes. Users are now able to decide whether they want to automatically change their linked file name when they change their entry data. LOGIC: JabRef can automatically renames linked files when entry data changes through an event-based system. Core implementation: AutomaticFileRenamer: Listens for entry changes, checks user preference before renaming  EnhancedFileRenamer: Executes renaming with pattern-based filenames FilePreferences: Stores the new preference setting (default: off) Key tests: AutomaticFileRenamerTest: Tests citation key change renaming, field change behavior EnhancedFileRenamerTest: Tests filename generation, conflict handling LinkedFileHandlerTest: Tests linked file operations with preference This feature maintains synchronization between bibliographic entries and filenames while giving users control over the automatic behavior. ps: for the case that multi linked files are linked to an entry, the current solution is to if the user change the entry data, all the linked files will change.  Closes [#11316](https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/issues/11316) ### Mandatory checks - [x] I own the copyright of the code submitted and I licence it under the [MIT license](https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/blob/main/LICENSE) - [ ] Change in `CHANGELOG.md` described in a way that is understandable for the average user (if change is visible to the user) - [x] Tests created for changes (if applicable) - [x] Manually tested changed features in running JabRef (always required) - [x] Screenshots added in PR description (for UI changes) - [x] [Checked developer's documentation](https://devdocs.jabref.org/): Is the information available and up to date? If not, I outlined it in this pull request. - [x] [Checked documentation](https://docs.jabref.org/): Is the information available and up to date? If not, I created an issue at or, even better, I submitted a pull request to the documentation repository.

UI:Add option to disable automatic file renaming on entry changes This PR adds a new user preference option to control whether files should be automatically renamed when entry details change: (below the linked file name convention is the button I added, auto rename files if entry data changed) FilesOnEntryChange (default: false) - Added new boolean preference in FilePreferences: autoRename - Added checkbox in Preferences > Linked Files settings to toggle this option - Modified AutomaticFileRenamer to respect this preference setting - Updated all relevant tests to accommodate this new preference This change gives users more control over when files are renamed, addressing concerns about unexpected file system changes. Users are now able to decide whether they want to automatically change their linked file name when they change their entry data. LOGIC: JabRef can automatically renames linked files when entry data changes through an event-based system. Core implementation: AutomaticFileRenamer: Listens for entry changes, checks user preference before renaming EnhancedFileRenamer: Executes renaming with pattern-based filenames FilePreferences: Stores the new preference setting (default: off) Key tests: AutomaticFileRenamerTest: Tests citation key change renaming, field change behavior EnhancedFileRenamerTest: Tests filename generation, conflict handling LinkedFileHandlerTest: Tests linked file operations with preference This feature maintains synchronization between bibliographic entries and filenames while giving users control over the automatic behavior. ps: for the case that multi linked files are linked to an entry, the current solution is to if the user change the entry data, all the linked files will change. Closes #11316 ### Mandatory checks - [x] I own the copyright of the code submitted and I licence it under the [MIT license](https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/blob/main/LICENSE) - [ ] Change in `CHANGELOG.md` described in a way that is understandable for the average user (if change is visible to the user) - [x] Tests created for changes (if applicable) - [x] Manually tested changed features in running JabRef (always required) - [x] Screenshots added in PR description (for UI changes) - [x] [Checked developer's documentation](https://devdocs.jabref.org/): Is the information available and up to date?
